questions.q: Why did the Constituent Assembly of India adopt the Preamble as the first part of the Constitution?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Preamble as the first part of the Constitution because it sets the tone for the entire document and highlights the fundamental principles of the Constitution.

questions.q: The Constitution of India is based on which one of the following principles?
What is the significance of the Fundamental Rights in the Constitution of India?
questions.answer: The Fundamental Rights are a unique feature of the Indian Constitution. They are a set of basic human rights guaranteed to all citizens by the Constitution. These rights are designed to protect the individual from the arbitrary powers of the state and to promote social justice.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Constitution of India is a _______________________ Constitution, as it has been amended 103 times since its enactment.
Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constitution of India?
questions.answer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constitution of India.

questions.q: What is the significance of the Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution of India?
questions.answer: The Directive Principles of State Policy are guidelines for the governance of the country. They aim to promote social and economic justice, equality, and fraternity. They provide a framework for the government to follow while making laws and policies.

questions.q: What is the difference between the Fundamental Rights and the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.answer: The Fundamental Rights are justiciable, meaning they can be enforced by the courts, whereas the Directive Principles of State Policy are not justiciable. They are guidelines for the government to follow, but they cannot be enforced by the courts.

questions.q: Why is the Constitution of India called a 'Living Document'?
questions.answer: The Constitution of India is called a 'Living Document' because it is a dynamic document that has been amended 103 times since its enactment. It is flexible and adaptable to the changing needs of the society. It has been able to survive and thrive for over seven decades due to its ability to evolve and change with the times.

questions.q: What is the significance of the Preamble in the Constitution of India?
questions.answer: The Preamble is the introduction to the Constitution of India. It sets the tone for the entire document and highlights the fundamental principles of the Constitution. It provides a framework for the governance of the country and serves as a guide for the interpretation of the Constitution.
